params.scenario: ★★★ SSE-AWARE capture for /rest/sse/perplexity_ask (Chrome HAR architectural limit — #743 verdict OK_extracted_partial revealed text/event-stream bodies are NOT captured by Chrome HAR exporter, only request+headers+size). Use one of: (a) DevTools 'Preserve log' + Network tab → click /rest/sse/perplexity_ask request → 'Response' tab → copy raw response chunks, (b) install fetch-intercept extension or use service worker proxy to log SSE events, (c) Chrome DevTools Protocol via remote debugging — Network.responseReceivedExtraInfo + Network.dataReceived events for the perplexity_ask request, (d) chrome://flags 'Enable HAR with content' experimental + retry HAR. Submit innocuous prompt 'What is 2+2?' and capture FULL response stream chunks. Goal: extract first 3-5 SSE chunks (event types, data field shape, sentinel terminator).
params.context: ★★★ V7-G perplexity SSE target — proposals/perplexity-V7-G-rest-sse-perplexity-ask-target-design-2026-05-07.md has {{HAR_REQUIRED}} placeholders awaiting SSE schema. #743 partial extract limits next iteration. SSE-aware capture is critical missing piece. Test PC subagent: use whichever of (a)-(d) is feasible, document method used. Output: JSON with 'capture_method' + 'sse_chunks[] {chunk_index, raw_text}' + 'event_types_observed[]' + 'terminator_pattern'. If all (a)-(d) fail, document specifically what failed for cause-based escalation.

notes: perplexity SSE body capture — #743 architectural limit (HAR no SSE body) requires alternative capture method. Subagent picks feasible method (DevTools Network tab response copy / DevTools Protocol / fetch intercept / experimental HAR-with-content).
